summary: Nocturnal hand pain suggests possible carpal tunnel syndrome; common tests include Phalen’s, Finkelstein’s, and Tinel’s signs which assess median nerve involvement. Excluding these, the Tourniquet test focuses on vascular assessment instead of neural function. \boxed{Tourniquet test}. Summary: Nighttime pain in fingers indicates potential carpal tunnel syndrome; typical tests involve Phalen's, Finkelstein's, and Tinel's signs targeting median nerve pressure. Since the Tourniket test checks circulation rather than nerve symptoms, it doesn't fit here.\boxed{Tourniket test}.
